title: Pseudo Substantiation of Clothes and Post 3I
display_title: Pseudo Substantiation of Clothes and Post 3I
wikitext:
[]: Pseudo-Substantiation of Clothes and Post-3I             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 6:48 pm In "One More Final: I Need You", Shinji and Asuka return from the LCL fully clothed. Furthermore, Asuka is wearing bandages that she did not have on her when she died, but the bandages she was wearing were covering the places where her injuries were. Thus, their clothes must have been formed by their minds. However , clothes are not part of the body and are not controlled by the AT Field... right? I mean, when everyone was popping, their clothes (and cyborg parts) remained. So, how were Shinji and Asuka clothed when they returned? Is it related to the pseudo-substantiation of Shinji's plugsuit in episode 20? Now, I've read that the "pseudo-substantiating his plugsuit" line was invented for the dub to account for a discrepancy in the fact that Shinji was not wearing his plugsuit during the previous fight. However, when I recently saw the episode subbed, that line was still there. Was that changed from the original subs? On the other hand, "pseudo-substantiation" would mean that the object doesn't actually exist, correct? That would explain why Shinji's clothes weren't dirty after weeks/months of not changing...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:05 pm The line is still there in Platinum's subs though: Episode 20 wrote: RITSUKO: All of the substances which composed Shinji are still preserved in the plug, and what could be called his soul exists there as well. In fact, his self-image is pseudo-substantiating his plug suit.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:08 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The line is still there in Platinum's subs though: Episode 20 wrote: RITSUKO: All of the substances which composed Shinji are still preserved in the plug, and what could be called his soul exists there as well. In fact, his self-image is pseudo-substantiating his plug suit.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:11 pm Er...wait. You mean the dub altered what the line was about? I've only watched a few scenes instead of episodes of the dub, so I didn't even know that. Interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:12 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Er...wait. You mean the dub altered what the line was about?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Aug 10, 2008 7:15 pm Well, since the force that rebuilt their body was a physical force of some type rearranging the molecular composition of LCL into a human body, and its directed by the "heart" of the person returning (I assume by "heart" they mean the soul), then making clothes from scratch out of LCL is a trivial matter. I would guess that the force at work is the AT field, which we know is a physical force in angels. There doesn't seem to me to be any other physical force that a disembodied soul has access to. And there is no reason that angel AT fields are any different from human ones, only in terms of scale. Otherwise they would not have the same name. Plus, they pick up GNR as a human by her AT field. That means that they are not any different, or else different equipment would be used to detect them. I said all this elsewhere. Take it as you will, but I think all of this should be obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Aug 10, 2008 10:27 pm I find it improbable that a complex device with a high non-organic material content - a plugsuit, a wristwatch, Misato's USP - could be recreated from LCL, particularly when it would be rather difficult for the owner to imagine said device's precise structure and matter composition mentally.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Aug 10, 2008 10:42 pm I think a human body is far more complex than a wristwatch, Bob. The human brain alone is the most highly organized collection of matter known to man. Then there are complex organs like the heart. Or the pancreas. Or even the skin . Sure, not everyone is an engineer. Nor do most people have the scientific knowledge to reconstruct even one human organ. But the fact is that somehow their souls were able to construct bodies that they should have no way of understanding, and that they came back with clothes that included bits of technology most people can't make on their own. DEAL WITH IT. Somehow they were able to do it, thats the entire point of the thread. Do YOU have a better explination for how Asuka's plugsuit got there with bandages? I really don't think you do. But I enjoy doing rebuttals! Edit: and BTW, LCL includes every chemical needed to construct a human being. That includes a certain proportion of heavy metals needed to construct technology. The soul just has to concentrate it in the reconstruction process.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Aug 10, 2008 10:54 pm Formless One wrote: Do YOU have a better explination for how Asuka's plugsuit got there with bandages?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:02 am The writers and animators couldn't be bothered to think it out, just as with the trees and the material thrown up by GNR's impacting head.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:26 am If the plugsuit's pseudo-substantiation line is indeed not made up for the dub, then there definitely is something to this. That is, unless that line was added in quickly after the animation was finished and, because of the rushed schedule, it was too late to change it once they realized that Shinji hadn't been wearing a plugsuit.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:41 am Well, even if the plugsuit was not originally intended to be floating in the LCL, that still doesn't explain EoE where Asuka returns in one and bandaged. Clearly if it was an error, they just decided to work with it after it was done, rather than removing it, which they could have done in the Platinum releases. After they put it into the movie, it was too late to take it back. Besides, the way the episode ends with having Misato crying over Shinji's plugsuit like it was a corpse seems like significant evidence that they meant for it to be like this from the start. That scene uses what Bob thinks of as an error by the animators for a purpose to the episode. Seems pretty intentional to me! What was Misato supposed to cry over? Spilled LCL? Also, what is the significance of the fact that when he returns from the plug the first thing that reforms is his plugsuit, yet in EoE after he has learned... whatever he learned ...he came back in normal clothes?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:46 am Formless One wrote: Also, what is the significance of the fact that when he returns from the plug the first thing that reforms is his plugsuit, yet in EoE after he has learned... whatever he learned ...he came back in normal clothes?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 11, 2008 12:52 am Yes. He did come back naked in the episode. However, that does not change the fact that the Plug suit formed in the plug after he went into the plug wearing normal clothes. BTW, just what happened to his normal clothes, anyway? They don't tang in instrumentality: but they seem to have vanished in this episode... However, I am now reminded that, since Misato was hugging the plugsuit which was clearly quite material, how was it a pseudo-substantiation and not an actual manifestation?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 11, 2008 1:07 am So if something not real stays there long enough, it becomes real? That kind of takes away a bit from the reality/dream message...

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 11, 2008 1:11 am No, it was his anchor to reality. It became more real in response to his desire to become real himself: it only becomes fully substantial right before he comes out himself. Make sense? That was my interpretation even before I thought about how people come back, BTW.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 11, 2008 1:21 am Please think of the cat girls.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 11, 2008 1:28 am I'm more of a foxgirl fan myself, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 11, 2008 1:31 am The basic physiology of Angels, along with the events of Instrumentality and episode 20, make it obvious to me that the souls has the ability to create matter out of LCL with it's AT Field, organic or otherwise. So long as it can imagine it within it's heart. I always figured that the plug suit was what manifested because Shinji associates his entire existence with his role as pilot. Perhaps he created it out of his left over clothes.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Aug 11, 2008 5:38 am He came back clothed because nobody wants to see Shinji in the buff. /thread
